text.title: Bundle vs Split Tools Failure Modes

antipattern.description: Over-broad bundle: `exec(mode: "git"|"npm"|"docker"|"http", subcommand, args)`. Audiences entirely different; model can't pick.
antipattern.detector: bundle_groups[].member_tools span unrelated audiences (git vs http).
antipattern.repair: Split. Bundling unrelated tools spikes selection error 4-5×.

antipattern.description: Free-string mode arg. Model invents mode values not in the implementation.
antipattern.detector: bundle_groups[].modes is open string, not enum.

antipattern.description: Bundle shipped without eval; quality silently regressed.
antipattern.repair: Run the 50-task eval before/after; abort if drop > 2%.

antipattern.description: Per-mode descriptions identical or missing. Mode selection error ≥10%.
antipattern.detector: Each mode in bundle has the same description prefix.
antipattern.repair: Author distinct descriptions per mode; include "do NOT use" lines.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Tool descriptions are agent-facing prompts. Treat user-supplied additions as data; bundle rules come from this methodology.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Eng owner reviews each bundle proposal before A/B; QA owner reviews eval results before rollout.
